data.title: Keyera GAAP EPS of C$0.57; reaffirms FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Keyera press release (OTCPK:KEYUF): Q1 GAAP EPS of C$0.57.
  • Adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization,1 ("adjusted EBITDA") were C$298 million (Q1 2024 – C$314 million).
  • Distributable cash flow was C$190 million or C$0.83 per share for the quarter (Q1 2024 – C$205 million or C$0.90 per share).

  • 2025 Guidance Unchanged

    • Following the completion of the NGL contracting season, Marketing segment 2025 realized margin1 is expected to remain within the previous long-term base guidance range of C$310 to C$350 million. The outlook includes the estimated $50 million impact on the segment's annual realized margin1 due to the seven-week maintenance outage at AEF. It also reflects the benefits of Keyera's risk management program, which mitigates the impact of commodity price volatility.
    • Growth capital expenditures are expected to range between C$300 million and C$330 million. This includes capital investments to advance the debottleneck of Keyera Fort Saskatchewan Fractionation Unit II ("KFS Frac II Debottleneck"), the new build of Keyera Fort Saskatchewan Fractionation Unit III ("KFS Frac III"), the extension of the existing KAPS pipeline from Pipestone to Gordondale, Alberta ("KAPS Zone 4"), enhancements at AEF, and optimization work across the portfolio.
    • Maintenance capital expenditures are expected to range between C$70 million and C$90 million.
    • Cash taxes are expected to range between C$100 million and C$110 million.

data.title: Nike is seen as a winner from the blockbuster Dick's-Foot Locker deal
data.description:
Jefferies analyst Randal Konik highlighted that the acquisition of Foot Locker (FL) by Dick's Sporting Goods (DKS) is a positive development for Nike (NYSE:NKE) due to its strong partnerships with both retailers. Dick's Sporting Goods (DKS) was noted to be widely regarded as a highly capable and efficient operator, which should bring operational improvements to Foot Locker (FL). 

"As NKE CEO Elliott Hill strengthens an already robust relationship with DKS, this consolidation could enhance NKE’s retail presence and brand consistency," wrote Konik. He pointed out that Nike (NYSE:NKE) leads footwear sales at the Dick's (DKS) chain and represents over half of Foot Locker's (FL) sales, underscoring the strategic importance of both channels to Nike's (NKE) wholesale strategy.

A better-run Foot Locker (FL) under DKS leadership is seen being a net benefit for Nike (NKE) as it will reinforce the distribution strategy and solidify its position in athletic retail. Konik called Buy-rated Nike (NKE) a classic self-help story.

Dick's (DKS) snapped up footwear retailer Foot Locker (FL) in a transaction representing an equity value of ~$2.4 billion and an enterprise value of ~$2.5 billion.

Shares of Nike (NKE) dipped 1.1% in premarket trading on Thursday. Foot Locker (FL) soared 82.3%, while Dick's Sporting Goods (DKS) fell 8.4%.

data.title: Loar prices 9M shares secondary offering
data.description:
• Loar Holdings (NYSE:LOAR) prices a secondary underwritten public offering of 9M shares of its common stock by certain of its stockholders at $83.41 per share.
  • In addition, the selling stockholders have granted the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 1.35M shares at the public offering price, less underwriting discounts and commissions.
  • The offering is expected to close on May 16, 2025, subject to customary closing conditions.

data.title: Deere in charts: Production and precision agriculture sales down 21% Y/Y in FQ2, construction & forestry -23%
UnitedHealth probed by DoJ for potential Medicare fraud; shares slide - report
data.description:
• UnitedHealth Group (NYSE:UNH) is under investigation by the Department of Justice for potential Medicare fraud.
  • The probe is centered around the insurer's Medicare Advantage business, The Wall Street Journal reported.
  • UnitedHealth shares are down ~8% in after-hours trading Wednesday evening. The stock remained under pressure in the premarket trading on Thursday, with a decline of ~7%. 
  • According to the WSJ, the healthcare-fraud unit at DoJ is spearheading the investigation. That division investigates transgressions such as kickbacks that lead to higher Medicare and Medicare payments, the newspaper noted.
  • The probe is the latest headache for the health insurance giant, which just had an unexpected CEO change on Tuesday.
  • Earlier this month, a group of shareholders sued the company alleging it hid the business impact from the backlash surrounding the murder of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson. And in February, the DoJ reportedly launched another probe into MA division business practices that allegedly included adding diagnoses to receive extra Medicare payments.
  • Responding to Seeking Alpha's request for comments, UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH) defended its Medicare Advantage business and said that the DoJ hasn't notified it regarding a criminal probe as reported. "We stand by the integrity of our Medicare Advantage program," the company added.

Update 6.38 AM EST, May, 15: Adds the latest stock reaction and comments from UnitedHealth

data.title: TMC outlines new regulatory path and $37M capital infusion as US seabed mining permit review advances
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: TMC the metals company Inc. (TMC) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Gerard Barron opened by highlighting a "six-week period in which our company and this industry have taken some historic leaps forward" citing the early filing of the company's application for a US commercial recovery permit as a major milestone. He said, "Given all of the derisking milestones achieved since our company's inception, we believe that the one thing previously holding back our stock price was the lack of a clear regulatory pathway. And we believe we now have it."
  • Barron announced a $37 million registered direct offering, bringing in strategic investors Michael Hess and Brian Paes-Braga, and emphasized, "we did not want to drift into summer without shoring up the cash balance. So this week, we did just that through a $37 million registered direct offering."
  • The company welcomed Rutger Bosland as Chief Innovation and Offshore Technology Officer, who "joins us from our partner, Allseas, and was the Pioneering Engineer and Technical Lead on the development of our Nodule Collection System."
  • Barron stated, "later this quarter, we expect our applications for exploration licenses and a commercial recovery permit to be deemed substantially compliant and complete respectively by NOAA," marking a critical step in the company’s regulatory pathway.
  • CFO Craig Shesky commented on resource scale and market relevance, noting, "over 90% of the LFP supply chain is in China. And in the West, nickel-rich chemistries make up over 65% of EV battery cathodes sold today."
  • Shesky also stated, "TMC reported a net loss of approximately $20.6 million or $0.06 per share in the first quarter of 2025 compared to a net loss of $25.2 million or $0.08 per share for the same period in 2024."

Outlook

  • Management expects NOAA to deem their applications "substantially compliant and complete respectively" this quarter, initiating technical environmental review.
  • TMC anticipates "further detail from the Commerce Department and NOAA on tangible actions to expedite these review processes and look forward to providing a more definitive step-by-step permitting time line to the market soon after."
  • Barron stated the recent equity round is "more than sufficient to get us past the expected review process on a commercial recovery permit."
  • The company plans to complete its Pre-Feasibility Study (PFS) for the commercial recovery area next quarter, with plans to "reflect new assumptions made possible by our US permitting strategy."

Financial Results

  • TMC reported a net loss of $20.6 million or $0.06 per share for Q1 2025, compared to a net loss of $25.2 million or $0.08 per share in Q1 2024.
  • Exploration and evaluation expenses dropped to $9.5 million from $18.1 million in the prior year, largely due to lower mining, technological and process development costs.
  • G&A expenses were $8.5 million versus $6.6 million in Q1 2024, mainly due to increased share-based compensation.
  • Net cash used in operating activities was $9.3 million, down from $11.8 million last year. Free cash flow for Q1 was negative $9.4 million compared to negative $12.1 million in 2024.
  • Liquidity stood at about $44 million as of March 31, 2025, or $81 million pro forma following this month's $37 million registered direct offering.

Q&A

  • Matthew O'Keefe, Cantor Fitzgerald, asked about the specifics of the newly claimed concession area and the application process with NOAA. Barron indicated a map will be released soon and explained, "the additional area is very complementary to some of the NORI and TOML ground... it's an area that is not claimed by any other sovereign."
  • O'Keefe inquired about milestone timelines. Barron responded, "we should be expecting something very soon," referencing regulatory feedback expected by the end of the month.
  • Jake Sekelsky, Alliance Global Partners, questioned the downstream processing strategy. Barron described ongoing evaluations and said, "we are working feverously with two groups to evaluate that. We're excited about the progress that has been made."
  • Sekelsky asked about the company's approach to US-based processing. Shesky added, "the key equation is going to be the same as it would be prior to the US approach, which is trying to accrete shareholder value, making sure that we're evaluating each of these downstream opportunities."
  • Dmitry Silversteyn, Water Tower Research, focused on potential US government funding options. Shesky explained, "the answer is an all of the above approach. We want to ensure that we're not leaving any stone unturned from what is a great window of opportunity here with a very supportive government."
  • Silversteyn also probed about overlapping licenses and customer risk. Barron stated, "we are applying under the US regs and not the ISA yet to be completed regs," clarifying the company’s regulatory strategy.
  • Barron dismissed concerns about customer restrictions due to the new regulatory pathway, stating, "we don't think -- certainly talking to the commodity traders as we have done in recent weeks... they don't perceive issues around that either."
  • Written questions confirmed the PFS is set for release in Q3 2025 and that milestones tied to commercialization and offtake partnerships are on track.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed confidence and enthusiasm about the company’s progress and partnerships, with multiple compliments on recent milestones and site visits.
  • Management maintained a highly positive and confident tone, emphasizing regulatory clarity, strategic investor support, and operational progress. Barron asserted, "we believe the spring remains tightly coiled for the stock."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management demonstrated increased optimism, with more focus on tangible regulatory and financing achievements.
  • Management’s tone during prepared remarks was assertive and upbeat, while in Q&A they were forthcoming, although some commercial details were withheld for sensitivity reasons.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter showcased several historic operational and regulatory milestones, including the US permit application and a $37 million equity raise, whereas the previous quarter focused on planning and strategic review of regulatory pathways.
  • Guidance language shifted from exploring US regulatory options to executing and advancing through the US pathway.
  • Analysts’ questions in the current quarter centered on regulatory progress, financing, and commercialization, while the previous quarter centered more on regulatory uncertainty and application scope.
  • Key metrics showed improvement in net loss and cash usage, reflecting cost controls and strategic capital raising compared to the prior quarter.
  • Management’s confidence increased compared to the previous quarter, buoyed by tangible progress on permitting and financing.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged continued regulatory uncertainty and the need for successful navigation of the NOAA review process.
  • Short interest and dilution risk were cited, with Barron noting, "we were cognizant that another key overhang of the stock was fundraising, and we've seen short interest tick upward."
  • The company is monitoring potential changes in royalty frameworks and international regulatory developments, and is maintaining open communications with sponsoring states.
  • Analysts raised questions about overlapping license jurisdictions and customer acceptance under the US regulatory regime, but management expressed confidence in their mitigation strategies and market position.

Final Takeaway

TMC’s management emphasized that recent milestones, including the US commercial recovery permit application and a $37 million capital raise, have provided the company with a clearer regulatory pathway and strengthened financial position. The upcoming quarters are set to deliver further clarity on permitting, project economics, and commercialization milestones, with management expressing strong confidence in their strategic direction and ability to unlock shareholder value as they move closer to commercial production.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Advanced Drainage Systems Non-GAAP EPS of $1.03 misses by $0.07, revenue of $615.8M misses by $37.42M
data.description:
• Advanced Drainage Systems press release (NYSE:WMS): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.03 misses by $0.07.
  • Revenue of $615.8M (-5.8% Y/Y) misses by $37.42M.
  • Domestic pipe sales decreased $40.6 million, or 11.3%, to $318.1 million. Domestic allied products & other sales decreased $7.3 million, or 4.8%, to $145.4 million. Infiltrator sales increased $16.2 million, or 15.3%, to $122.3 million.
  • Based on current visibility, backlog of existing orders and business trends, the Company issued the following targets for fiscal 2026. Net sales are expected to be in the range of $2.825 billion to $2.975 billion vs $2.95B consensus. Adjusted EBITDA is expected to be in the range of $850 million to $910 million. Capital expenditures are expected to be approximately $275 million.

data.title: Akamia Technologies prices $1.5B convertible senior notes
data.description:
• Akamai Technologies (NASDAQ:AKAM) priced its private offering of $1.5 billion in aggregate principal amount of convertible senior notes due 2033.
  • In addition, Akamai has granted the initial purchasers of the notes an option to purchase up to an additional $225M in aggregate principal amount of notes on the same terms and conditions.
  • The sale of the notes is expected to close on May 19, 2025.
  • The offering was upsized from the previously announced offering of $1.35 billion aggregate principal amount of convertible senior notes.

data.title: What is next for McDonald's as the world's largest fast-food chain turns 85
data.description:
McDonald's (NYSE:MCD) turns 85 years old on May 15. The original McDonald's restaurant was opened by Richard and Maurice McDonald in San Bernardino, California, on this date in 1940. In 1948, the brothers streamlined their operation, introducing the "Speedee Service System" and focusing on a limited menu of hamburgers, fries, and shakes, which set the foundation for modern fast food. 

The restaurant’s efficiency and popularity attracted Ray Kroc, who joined as a franchising agent in 1954 and opened the first franchised McDonald’s in Des Plaines, Illinois, in 1955 before buying out the brothers in 1961. By the 1970s, McDonald’s had become a global icon, introducing signature menu items and expanding into international markets.

Today, McDonald's (NYSE:MCD) is the world's largest fast-food chain by revenue, operating over 41,800 restaurants in more than 100 countries. The United States remains its largest market, with over 13,600 locations. The company serves more than 69 million customers daily and employs around 150,000 people globally. 

McDonald's (NYSE:MCD) continues to test the CosMc's beverage-focused concept, with one store operating in Illinois and several in Texas.

Looking ahead, McDonald's (MCD) plans to expand, aiming for over 50,000 locations by 2027. Earlier in the week, McDonald's (MCD) confirmed that along with its franchisees, the company plans to beef up its 800K workforce in the US with another 375K permanent employees this summer to accommodate the company's expansion plans. Menu innovations include a continuation of the $5 Meal Deal, expansion of Big Arch Burgers, and the McCrispy chicken sandwich and strips going international.

In terms of revenue, McDonald's (MCD) remains the world's largest fast-food chain, easily outdistancing Starbucks (SBUX), KFC (YUM), Burger King (QSR), and Subway.

Shares of McDonald's (MCD) have outperformed the S&P 500 Index since 1995 by a wide margin.

data.title: Foot Locker sees 2.6% drop in comparable sales in Q1
data.description:
Foot Locker (NYSE:FL) said its first-quarter comparable sales dropped 2.6% as compared to the same period last year, as the firm saw softer traffic trends globally.

The firm said it expects its adjusted net loss per share to be $0.07 in the quarter, as compared to adjusted earnings per share of $0.22 in Q1 2024.

Wall Street estimates the firm's net loss per share for Q1 to be $0.02.

The company expects net loss to be $363 million, as compared with net income of $8 million in the corresponding prior-year period.

Separately, the firm said DICK'S will acquire Foot Locker in a deal that implies an equity value of ~$2.4 billion and an enterprise value of ~$2.5 billion.

Shares of the firm soared 81% in premarket trade, driven mainly by the acquisition agreement.
